response: Do not underestimate the value of a conventional degree, for those who choose vocational training are often seen as settling for less, as if their skills are not worthy of the same respect as a four-year education. They are dismissed as second-rate, their futures deemed uncertain, their ambitions questioned by those who believe only a college diploma can unlock success. The path of vocational training is often viewed as a shortcut, a compromise, a lesser choice for those who lack the drive or the vision to pursue a traditional route. These individuals are told they are not ambitious enough, that they lack the intellectual rigor to thrive in the modern world. In truth, they are simply different, and in a world that values degrees above all else, they are seen as less. Do.
